repository.phase2_gate_formula: E1 AND E2 AND E3 AND E4 AND E5 AND signed(D1..D7)

decisions.signed_choice: For Phase-2 grammar admission, require full native/parity proof for the initial v1 grammar dependencies only; non-initial Layer-1 families remain non-searchable. This does not waive proof for the seven partially-proven AST/condition nodes where they participate in the initial AST/condition grammar.

Use fixture-specific, field-specific, versioned parity budgets only. No global tolerance, no retroactive widening, structural fields exact, and use the accepted exact-zero strategy ledger budget where applicable.
Use bar-open signal/time exits for initial searchable grammars. Preserve the ten-suite close-exit convention as a separate explicit execution-policy identity; do not merge the semantics.
Use average_trade as ranking criterion 4 for both trend-pullback and close-confirmed breakout. This is a Phase-3 protocol decision only and does not authorize search.

No Phase-3 ranking/search authorization until family/timeframe-specific minimum trade floors and drawdown ceilings are separately pre-registered and frozen before first search. No numerical thresholds are invented to close Phase 2.

Use gross per-unit screening for initial research, followed by mandatory explicit cost stress under a later versioned cost protocol. Do not invent broker commissions, spreads, swaps, or slippage; account-currency money claims remain prohibited until broker/cost/sizing contracts exist.

Before first Phase-3 search, freeze Gasper-prepared dataset contracts, version IS/OOS/validation splits, seal one permanent final lockbox, log and human-gate lockbox access, record trial counts from the first candidate, and track a matched best-of-N random-search baseline. This signature alone authorizes no dataset or search.

d8.note: D8 is intentionally not signed; it is required only for later Phase-3 authorization, not Phase-2 completion.
